description.value:
You crumble a small piece of bark in your hand and transform one or both of your arms into massive branches or trunks. One or both of your hands takes the shape of a thick trunk that can be used to make unarmed attacks. This attack deals 1d8 bludgeoning damage, has the grapple and sweep traits, and is in the club group. Small roots extend from the end of the trunk and allow you to Interact with objects, but you cannot wield a weapon in this hand. Instead of the normal critical specialization effect, if you roll a critical success on an attack with arboreal arms the small roots at the end of the trunk extend out and wrap around some part of your foe's body; you may make a Grapple attempt as a free action. This Grapple attempt doesn't count toward your multiple attack penalty, and your multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Grapple check. This unarmed attack is a valid target for shillelagh.

The duration increases to 10 minutes and you can Dismiss the spell to end its duration early.

You crumble a small piece of bark over your chest and it morphs into a thick bole. Your chest is covered in layers of thick heartwood and bark. You gain a number of temporary Hit Points equal to twice your level. You gain the effects of a 6th-rank vital beacon that lasts for the duration of this spell.

You crumble a small piece of bark onto your legs and they become covered in bark and take on the power of supple trees. For the duration you are immune to forced movement, unless you are willing to be moved. When you take the Leap action you may jump up to your Speed or 30 feet, whichever is higher, in any direction. You must land on a space of solid ground or else you fall after using your next action. If your next action after Leaping is a Strike you impart the energy of your leap into your attack; the Strike gains a circumstance bonus to damage equal to 2 per damage die of your weapon or unarmed attack. You can exert a great deal of control over this transformation. You may choose to create truly massive arboreal legs, increasing your size to Large. If you do so, this spell gains the polymorph trait, and your reach increases by 5 feet (or by 10 feet if you started out tiny). If you are also activating arboreal arms or arboreal bole when casting this spell, you may choose to instead become Huge, or Gargantuan if you are activating all three. If you become Huge, your reach increases by 10 feet, or 15 feet if you become Gargantuan (15 and 20 feet respectively if you started out tiny). You can Dismiss this effect.

You conjure the vengeance of shattered trees in the form of a splintered shaft of wood that you launch toward your foe. Make a spell attack roll. On a hit, you deal 6d6 piercing damage and 1d6 persistent bleed damage.

Increase the piercing damage by 2d6, and the persistent bleed by 1d6.

You cause a web of hardened vines to grow up out of the ground around a creature, protecting them from attacks. Each square adjacent to the target becomes difficult terrain for the duration of the spell, and the target gains resistance 10 to slashing and bludgeoning damage as long as they stay in the same square. After the spell has blocked 30 damage, it ends immediately.

Increase the resistance granted by 2, and the total amount blocked by 10.

You take the form of a powerful arboreal, a living, walking tree, assuming a Huge battle form. Your arms become branches and your fingers become twigs with leaves. Your legs are like the trunks of a tree. While in this form you gain the plant trait. You can speak while in this form. You can Dismiss this spell. You gain the following statistics and abilities:

  • AC = 21 + your level. Ignore your armor's check penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 15 temporary Hit Points.
  • Speed 40 feet.
  • Resistance 10 to physical.
  • Darkvision and tremorsense 60 feet.
  • Athletics modifier of +30, unless your own modifier is higher.
You gain the following melee attacks, which are the only attacks you can use. You are trained with them. Your attack modifier is +28, and you use the listed damage. These attacks are Strength based (for the purpose of the enfeebled condition, for example). If your unarmed attack modifier is higher, you can use it instead. Melee [one-action] branch (reach 20 feet), Damage 3d12 + 15 bludgeoning Melee [one-action] trunk (agile, reach 15 feet), Damage 3d8 + 15 bludgeoning The following text is the property of Wizards of the Coast, Inc. and is Copyright 2000 Wizards of the Coast, Inc ("Wizards").
